title: hypericum ovalifolium subsp. ovalifolium
intro: Hypericum ovalifolium subsp. ovalifolium, also known as small St. John's wort or creeping St. John's wort, is a species of flowering plant in the family Hypericaceae. It is a low-growing, evergreen perennial herb with oval-shaped leaves and yellow flowers that have a high concentration of bioactive compounds, including flavonoids and naphthoquinones, which possess medicinal properties. The plant's medicinal properties include anti-inflammatory, antimicrobial, and antioxidant effects, which contribute to its health benefits, such as wound healing, skin protection, and potential anti-depressant effects. The plant is used in herbal preparations, including teas, tinctures, and creams, which are used to treat various health conditions, including skin irritations, wounds, and mild depression.
